name: Ubuntu
description: The world's most popular Linux distro. Polished GNOME desktop, excellent hardware support, massive community. The best starting point for newcomers.

name: Kubuntu
description: Ubuntu with KDE Plasma desktop. Highly configurable, modern UI, and strong performance with a traditional desktop workflow.

name: Xubuntu
description: Lightweight Ubuntu flavor using XFCE. Fast, stable, and ideal for older hardware or users wanting a simple desktop.

name: Lubuntu
description: Ultra-light Ubuntu flavor using LXQt. Designed for minimal resource usage and fast performance on low-end systems.

name: Ubuntu Studio
description: Ubuntu flavor tailored for audio, video, and graphics production. Ships with professional creative tools preinstalled.

name: Ubuntu Cinnamon
description: Ubuntu with the Cinnamon desktop environment, offering a traditional, Windows-like layout with modern Linux stability.

name: Ubuntu MATE
description: Ubuntu with the MATE desktop, continuing the GNOME 2 legacy. Lightweight, stable, and highly traditional in workflow.

name: Ubuntu Unity
description: Community continuation of Ubuntu's original Unity desktop. Vertical launcher, global menu, and a unique workflow design.

name: UbuntuDDE Remix
description: Community Ubuntu-based remix that ships with the Deepin Desktop Environment (DDE).

name: KDE neon
description: Ubuntu-based distribution showcasing the latest upstream KDE Plasma releases directly from the KDE project. Ideal for users who want cutting-edge KDE without waiting for distro release cycles.

name: Linux Lite
description: Ubuntu-based lightweight distribution designed for Windows users transitioning to Linux. Focuses on simplicity, low resource usage, and ease of use.

name: Linux Mint
description: Linux that just works. Based on Ubuntu LTS, Mint's Cinnamon DE feels instantly familiar to Windows users. The community's favourite recommendation for switchers.

name: Bodhi Linux
description: Minimal Ubuntu-based distribution using the Moksha desktop (Enlightenment fork). Focused on lightweight performance and modular user customization.

name: PureOS
description: Privacy-focused Debian-based distribution developed by Purism. Emphasizes free software, security, and user privacy by default.

name: Arco Linux
description: Arch-based distribution designed for learning Arch Linux. Provides multiple editions and progressive learning paths from beginner to advanced users.

name: RebornOS
description: Arch-based distribution focused on modular installation and customization. Provides a GUI installer and optional components for tailored systems.

name: BlackArch Linux
description: Arch-based penetration testing and security distribution containing thousands of security tools. Designed for advanced security research and ethical hacking.

name: BackBox Linux
description: Ubuntu-based penetration testing distribution focused on security analysis, network assessment, and forensic tools in a lightweight XFCE environment.

name: antiX
description: Ultra-lightweight Debian-based distribution designed for older hardware and systemd-free environments. Extremely resource-efficient and fast.

name: Linux Mint Debian Edition
description: Debian-based variant of Linux Mint designed as a fallback in case Ubuntu ever becomes unsuitable. Maintains Mint's Cinnamon desktop experience on a Debian base.

name: GNU Guix System
description: Functional Linux distribution using the GNU Guix package manager. Provides reproducible system configuration and transactional system management similar to NixOS.

name: Debian
description: The universal operating system. Debian's legendary stability comes from an exhaustive testing process — packages are glacially slow to update, but never break.

name: Fedora Workstation
description: Pure upstream GNOME experience. Fedora ships the latest kernel, drivers, and software — excellent for developers who want cutting-edge without rolling chaos.

name: Pop!_OS
description: Made by System76 for their hardware but excellent anywhere. COSMIC is their new Rust-based DE. Superb GPU driver support — loved by gamers and developers.

name: NixOS
description: Purely functional Linux. Your entire system declared in one config file — atomic upgrades, rollbacks, and reproducible builds. The future, but with homework.

name: openSUSE Tumbleweed
description: Rolling release with the reliability of the openSUSE testing pipeline. Tumbleweed gives you the latest software while still feeling mature and tested.

name: Manjaro
description: Arch Linux made accessible. Manjaro buffers Arch packages by ~2 weeks for extra stability, while still providing AUR access and a rolling experience.

name: Arch Linux
description: You build it, you own it. Arch is a blank canvas — install exactly what you need. Rolling release, AUR access, and the legendary Arch Wiki as your guide.

name: EndeavourOS
description: Arch, but with a friendly installer. EndeavourOS gives you a near-vanilla Arch experience with a Calamares GUI installer. Community-driven and welcoming.

name: Garuda Linux
description: Arch-based with performance tweaks out of the box. BTRFS snapshots, auto-rollback, gaming optimisations, and stunning KDE themes. Eye-candy meets performance.

name: Ubuntu Server
description: The most popular Linux server OS. Rock-solid LTS releases with 5-year support, massive community, and excellent cloud integration.

name: Fedora Server
description: Fedora's server edition ships cutting-edge tech ahead of RHEL. Ideal for developers who want modern server tech with a fast release cycle.

name: openSUSE Leap
description: Enterprise-grade stability from the openSUSE project. Binary compatible with SUSE Linux Enterprise, perfect for serious server deployments.

name: Rocky Linux
description: RHEL-compatible enterprise Linux, community-driven. The spiritual successor to CentOS — bulletproof stability for production server workloads.

name: Red Hat Enterprise Linux
description: The upstream enterprise Linux standard. Red Hat Enterprise Linux powers most commercial server infrastructure and defines the ecosystem that CentOS Stream, Rocky, and Alma follow.

name: CentOS Stream
description: The rolling preview of Red Hat Enterprise Linux. CentOS Stream sits between Fedora and RHEL, providing a forward-looking but still enterprise-aligned server platform.

name: SteamOS
description: Valve's gaming-focused Linux distribution powering the Steam Deck. Immutable design, optimized drivers, and a console-like desktop experience.

name: Artix Linux
description: Arch-based distribution without systemd. Artix gives you full Arch control while using alternative init systems like OpenRC, runit, or s6.

name: Solus
description: Independent rolling release distro focused on desktop usability. Known for the Budgie desktop and a curated, cohesive user experience.

name: Clear Linux
description: Intel-optimized Linux distribution focused on extreme performance. Aggressively tuned compiler flags, stateless design, and fast updates.

name: Pika OS
description: Debian-based but with a bleeding-edge gaming focus. PikaOS ships a patched kernel, up-to-date Mesa, and Wayland-first defaults — a gamer's shortcut to a working rig.

name: Gentoo
description: Compile everything from source, tune every USE flag, own every byte. Gentoo rewards patience with an intimately understood system and unmatched flexibility.

name: Slackware
description: The oldest surviving Linux distro. Slackware is intentionally hand-crafted — no package manager dependency resolution, no hand-holding. Pure Unix philosophy.

name: Void Linux
description: Independent rolling distro with runit init, xbps package manager, and musl libc support. Lightweight, fast, and entirely self-sufficient — no systemd, no Debian, no Arch.

name: openSUSE MicroOS
description: Immutable, transactional-update desktop from openSUSE. The root filesystem is read-only; changes apply on reboot via snapshots. Unbreakable by design.

name: Fedora Silverblue
description: Fedora's immutable desktop. The OS image is read-only and updated atomically — apps live in Flatpak or containers. Rollback is one command away.

name: Zorin OS
description: The most polished Linux for Windows and macOS switchers. Zorin's layout switcher mimics Windows 11, macOS, or classic Windows — pick your comfort zone.

name: elementary OS
description: macOS-inspired design meets open source. elementary OS ships its own Pantheon desktop, a curated AppCenter, and a strong no-telemetry privacy stance.

name: MX Linux
description: Consistently the most downloaded distro on DistroWatch. MX Linux pairs Debian Stable with antiX's live tools, its own MX Tools GUI suite, and a welcoming forum.

name: Kali Linux
description: The gold standard for penetration testing. 600+ security tools pre-installed, built on Debian Testing. Not for daily driving — purpose-built for offensive security work.

name: Alpine Linux
description: Tiny, security-focused, musl-based Linux. The go-to base for Docker containers — a full system under 10MB. Ideal for embedded systems, VMs, and minimal servers.

name: Alma Linux
description: A 1:1 RHEL-compatible rebuild, community-owned and free forever. AlmaLinux is the drop-in CentOS replacement for enterprise server workloads.

name: Tails
description: Privacy and anonymity, no traces left behind. Tails routes all traffic through Tor, runs entirely in RAM, and forgets everything on shutdown. The journalist's and activist's choice.

name: Qubes OS
description: Security through compartmentalisation. Every app runs in its own Xen VM — compromise one, and the rest of your system stays clean. Edward Snowden's daily driver.

name: CachyOS
description: Arch-based and obsessively performance-tuned. CachyOS ships a custom BORE/EEVDF scheduler kernel, x86-64-v3 optimised packages, and a polished Calamares installer.

name: Bazzite
description: Fedora-based immutable gaming OS. Bazzite is what SteamOS wishes it was on desktop — pre-configured for Wayland gaming, HDR, handheld controllers, and atomic updates.

name: openSUSE Aeon
description: Immutable GNOME desktop from openSUSE. Minimal base, transactional updates, Flatpak for everything. Set it up once, let it maintain itself forever.

name: KaOS
description: A KDE Plasma–focused independent Linux distribution. KaOS is Qt-first, rolling release, and tightly curated for a pure KDE experience.

name: Q4OS
description: Lightweight Debian-based distribution designed to be extremely fast and Windows-friendly, with a classic Trinity or KDE Plasma desktop.

name: Puppy Linux
description: Ultra-lightweight Linux distribution designed to run entirely in RAM. Extremely fast and ideal for old hardware or rescue systems.

name: Peppermint OS
description: Lightweight Debian-based desktop focused on web integration and simplicity. Designed for low-resource machines and cloud-first workflows.

name: PCLinuxOS
description: User-friendly rolling distribution with its own APT-RPM package system. Known for simplicity and strong community focus.

name: OpenMandriva Lx
description: Independent community-driven continuation of Mandriva Linux. Focused on KDE Plasma and modern toolchains.

name: Netrunner
description: Debian-based KDE distribution focused on usability and multimedia readiness. Ships with a curated desktop experience.

name: Mageia
description: Community-driven fork of Mandriva Linux. Focuses on stability, simplicity, and strong system tools.

name: Mabox Linux
description: Manjaro-based lightweight Openbox desktop with extensive configuration tools. Focused on minimalism and speed.

name: Knoppix
description: One of the original live Linux distributions. Runs entirely from USB/DVD without installation, widely used for rescue and recovery.

name: Parrot OS
description: Security-focused Debian-based distribution designed for penetration testing, forensics, and privacy. Includes a large suite of security and cryptography tools out of the box.

name: Tiny Core Linux
description: Ultra-minimal Linux distribution designed to be extremely small and modular. Core system can be under 20MB with everything else added as extensions.
